content: Which of the following is used to prepare the inner lining of a blast furnace?
explanation: Fire clay bricks is used to prepare the inner lining of a blast furnace. It is highly refractory in nature and does not melt, even at huge temperature. Its main components are silica and alumina.

content: The reducing agent which is used to reduce iron oxide in blast furnace is
explanation:
Carbon monoxide (CO) is the reducing agent which reduces iron oxide (Fe$$_2$$O$$_3$$) to iron in the blast furnace.

$$\mathrm{F{e_2}{O_3} + 3CO \to 2Fe + 3C{O_2} \uparrow}$$

content:
During the extraction of gold the following reactions take place

$$\mathrm{Au + C{N^ - } + {H_2}O\buildrel {{O_2}} \over \longrightarrow [X]}$$

$$\mathrm{[X] + Zn\buildrel {} \over \longrightarrow [Y] + Au}$$

X and Y are respectively
explanation:
During the extraction of gold by the cyanide process, the reactions involved are as follows:

  1. The gold is first leached out of the ore using a sodium cyanide solution. This forms a soluble gold cyanide complex.

    $ \text{Au} + 2\text{CN}^- + \text{H}_2\text{O} + \frac{1}{2}\text{O}_2 \longrightarrow \text{Au(CN)}_2^- + 2\text{OH}^- $

    In this reaction, $ \text{X} $ is $ \text{Au(CN)}_2^- $.

  2. Zinc is then used to displace gold from the gold cyanide complex:

    $ \text{Au(CN)}_2^- + \text{Zn} \longrightarrow \text{Zn(CN)}_4^{2-} + \text{Au} $

    In this reaction, $ \text{Y} $ is $ \text{Zn(CN)}_4^{2-} $.

Therefore, $ \text{X} $ and $ \text{Y} $ are $ \text{Au(CN)}_2^- $ and $ \text{Zn(CN)}_4^{2-} $ respectively.

The correct option is :

Option A : $ \text{[Au(CN)}_2\text{]}^- $ and $ \text{[Zn(CN)}_4\text{]}^{2-} $
Ellingham diagram represent a graph of $$\Delta G^\circ$$ vs T. It shows the temperature dependence of the stability of compound.

The ore not containing iron is carnallite.

It's formula is KCl . MgCl$$_2$$ . 6H$$_2$$O
